Calculation started earlier: the frontier-orbitals task launched with PySCF writing its live log to a `frontier_<timestamp>.out` file in the working directory (tail-f available during the run).  

Method provenance
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans-style orbital energy difference, eV).  
Level of theory: tier `standard` → B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted) DFT.  
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied geometry at /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/formaldehyde/formaldehyde.xyz (no re-optimization; i.e. DFT//user-provided).  
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation).  
Charge / multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet, RKS).  
Software: PySCF (backend invoked via chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill).  
Convergence & warnings: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no warnings reported by the engine.  

Result (gas phase, B3LYP/def2-TZVP):  
• HOMO energy = −7.6523067 eV  
• LUMO energy = −1.7826355 eV  
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 5.8696712 eV  

integrity.trustworthy verdict from chemkit: true
